revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 6.2 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 28.89 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂}} (Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto)
Mizusasara-hime-no-Mikoto (弥豆佐佐良比賣命) |type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime no Yashiro (度会之大国玉姫の社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 20) |Rituals = }} '''Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine''' (度会大国玉比賣神社, *Watarai Ōkunitamahi-me Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is located in the forested area of the Outer Shrine precincts, to the southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiyasaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫}}. [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] == Overview == {{ill|Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社}} is located within the sacred forest ({{ill|Jingū-rin|ja|神宮林}}) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is not directly connected to the main approach (参道) of the precinct, but its entrance can be found along the boundary of the Gekū's sacred grounds. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it ranks fourth, following {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社、大間国生神社}} and {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社}}Sakurai (1998): p. 85. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Kunitama|ja|大国玉命|lt=Ōkunidama no Mikoto}} and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|弥豆佐佐良比賣命}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): p. 37, both considered the {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|地主}} (local tutelary deities) of the Watarai region. It is said that when {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造}}, visited to pacify Ise during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, these two deities welcomed him by creating a bridge with their bows. Notably, Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto’s son, {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦国見賀岐建与束命}}, is enshrined at Watarai Kunimi Shrine. The precinct is surrounded by dense, towering trees, creating an atmosphere of deep shade even during the day. The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}}), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue wareIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫}} site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]] == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23年3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[en:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]

{{Shrine |Name = Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 6.2 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 28.89 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂}} (Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto)
Mizusasara-hime-no-Mikoto (弥豆佐佐良比賣命) |type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime no Yashiro (度会之大国玉姫の社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 20) |Rituals = }} '''Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine''' (度会大国玉比賣神社, *Watarai Ōkunitamahi-me Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is located in the forested area of the Outer Shrine precincts, to the southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiyasaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫}}. [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] == Overview == {{ill|Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社}} is located within the sacred forest ({{ill|Jingū-rin|ja|神宮林}}) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is not directly connected to the main approach (参道) of the precinct, but its entrance can be found along the boundary of the Gekū's sacred grounds. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it ranks fourth, following {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社、大間国生神社}} and {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社}}Sakurai (1998): p. 85. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Kunitama|ja|大国玉命|lt=Ōkunidama no Mikoto}} and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|弥豆佐佐良比賣命}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): p. 37, both considered the {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|地主}} (local tutelary deities) of the Watarai region. It is said that when {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, visited to pacify Ise during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, these two deities welcomed him by creating a bridge with their bows. Notably, Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto’s son, {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦国見賀岐建与束命}}, is enshrined at Watarai Kunimi Shrine. The precinct is surrounded by dense, towering trees, creating an atmosphere of deep shade even during the day. The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}}), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue wareIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫}} site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]] == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23年3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[en:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]]

The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}}), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue wareIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫}} site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]] == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23年3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[en:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{Shrine |Name = Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 6.2 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 28.89 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂}} (Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto)
Mizusasara-hime-no-Mikoto (弥豆佐佐良比賣命) |type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime no Yashiro (度会之大国玉姫の社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 20) |Rituals = }} '''Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine''' (度会大国玉比賣神社, *Watarai Ōkunitamahi-me Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is located in the forested area of the Outer Shrine precincts, to the southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiyasaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫}}. [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] == Overview == {{ill|Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社}} is located within the sacred forest ({{ill|Jingū-rin|ja|神宮林}}) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is not directly connected to the main approach (参道) of the precinct, but its entrance can be found along the boundary of the Gekū's sacred grounds. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it ranks fourth, following {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社、大間国生神社}} and {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社}}Sakurai (1998): p. 85. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Kunitama|ja|大国玉命|lt=Ōkunidama no Mikoto}} and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|弥豆佐佐良比賣命}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): p. 37, both considered the {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|地主}} (local tutelary deities) of the Watarai region. It is said that when {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, visited to pacify Ise during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, these two deities welcomed him by creating a bridge with their bows. Notably, Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto’s son, {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦国見賀岐建与束命}}, is enshrined at Watarai Kunimi Shrine. The precinct is surrounded by dense, towering trees, creating an atmosphere of deep shade even during the day. The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}}), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue wareIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫}} site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[en:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 6.2 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 28.89 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂}} (Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto)
Mizusasara-hime-no-Mikoto (弥豆佐佐良比賣命) |type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime no Yashiro (度会之大国玉姫の社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 20) |Rituals = }} '''Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine''' (度会大国玉比賣神社, *Watarai Ōkunitamahi-me Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is located in the forested area of the Outer Shrine precincts, to the southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiyasaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫}}. [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] == Overview == {{ill|Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社}} is located within the sacred forest ({{ill|Jingū-rin|ja|神宮林}}) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is not directly connected to the main approach (参道) of the precinct, but its entrance can be found along the boundary of the Gekū's sacred grounds. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it ranks fourth, following {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社、大間国生神社}} and {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社}}Sakurai (1998): p. 85. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Kunitama|ja|大国玉命|lt=Ōkunidama no Mikoto}} and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|弥豆佐佐良比賣命}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): p. 37, both considered the {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|地主}} (local tutelary deities) of the Watarai region. It is said that when {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, visited to pacify Ise during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, these two deities welcomed him by creating a bridge with their bows. Notably, Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto’s son, {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦国見賀岐建与束命}}, is enshrined at Watarai Kunimi Shrine. The precinct is surrounded by dense, towering trees, creating an atmosphere of deep shade even during the day. The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}}), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫}} site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]] == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23年3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[en:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]]

{{Shrine |Name = Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 6.2 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 28.89 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂}} (Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto)
Mizusasara-hime-no-Mikoto (弥豆佐佐良比賣命) |type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime no Yashiro (度会之大国玉姫の社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 20) |Rituals = }} '''Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine''' (度会大国玉比賣神社, *Watarai Ōkunitamahi-me Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is located in the forested area of the Outer Shrine precincts, to the southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiyasaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫}}. [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] == Overview == {{ill|Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社}} is located within the sacred forest ({{ill|Jingū-rin|ja|神宮林}}) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is not directly connected to the main approach (参道) of the precinct, but its entrance can be found along the boundary of the Gekū's sacred grounds. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it ranks fourth, following {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社、大間国生神社}} and {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社}}Sakurai (1998): p. 85. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Kunitama|ja|大国玉命|lt=Ōkunidama no Mikoto}} and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|弥豆佐佐良比賣命}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): p. 37, both considered the {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|地主}} (local tutelary deities) of the Watarai region. It is said that when {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, visited to pacify Ise during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, these two deities welcomed him by creating a bridge with their bows. Notably, Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto’s son, {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦国見賀岐建与束命}}, is enshrined at Watarai Kunimi Shrine. The precinct is surrounded by dense, towering trees, creating an atmosphere of deep shade even during the day. The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}}), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫}} site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]] == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23年3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[en:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]]

The precinct is surrounded by dense, towering trees, creating an atmosphere of deep shade even during the day. The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}}), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫}} site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]] == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23年3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4== See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[en:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]]

{{Shrine |Name = Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 6.2 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 28.89 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂}} (Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto)
Mizusasara-hime-no-Mikoto (弥豆佐佐良比賣命) |type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime no Yashiro (度会之大国玉姫の社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 20) |Rituals = }} '''Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine''' (度会大国玉比賣神社, *Watarai Ōkunitamahi-me Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is located in the forested area of the Outer Shrine precincts, to the southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiyasaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫}}. [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] == Overview == {{ill|Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社}} is located within the sacred forest ({{ill|Jingū-rin|ja|神宮林}}) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is not directly connected to the main approach (参道) of the precinct, but its entrance can be found along the boundary of the Gekū's sacred grounds. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it ranks fourth, following {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社、大間国生神社}} and {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社}}Sakurai (1998): p. 85. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Kunitama|ja|大国玉命|lt=Ōkunidama no Mikoto}} and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|弥豆佐佐良比賣命}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): p. 37, both considered the {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|地主}} (local tutelary deities) of the Watarai region. It is said that when {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, visited to pacify Ise during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, these two deities welcomed him by creating a bridge with their bows. Notably, Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto’s son, {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦国見賀岐建与束命}}, is enshrined at Watarai Kunimi Shrine. The precinct is surrounded by dense, towering trees, creating an atmosphere of deep shade even during the day. The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}}), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫}} site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]] == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23年3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4== See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[en:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also! == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[en:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also! ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 6.2 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 28.89 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂}} (Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto)
Mizusasara-hime-no-Mikoto (弥豆佐佐良比賣命) |type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime no Yashiro (度会之大国玉姫の社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 20) |Rituals = }} '''Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine''' (度会大国玉比賣神社, *Watarai Ōkunitamahi-me Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is located in the forested area of the Outer Shrine precincts, to the southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiyasaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫}}. [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] == Overview == {{ill|Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社}} is located within the sacred forest ({{ill|Jingū-rin|ja|神宮林}}) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is not directly connected to the main approach (参道) of the precinct, but its entrance can be found along the boundary of the Gekū's sacred grounds. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it ranks fourth, following {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社、大間国生神社}} and {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社}}Sakurai (1998): p. 85. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Kunitama|ja|大国玉命|lt=Ōkunidama no Mikoto}} and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|弥豆佐佐良比賣命}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): p. 37, both considered the {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|地主}} (local tutelary deities) of the Watarai region. It is said that when {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, visited to pacify Ise during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, these two deities welcomed him by creating a bridge with their bows. Notably, Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto’s son, {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦国見賀岐建与束命}}, is enshrined at Watarai Kunimi Shrine. The precinct is surrounded by dense, towering trees, creating an atmosphere of deep shade even during the day. The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}}), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫}} site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]] == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23年3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[en:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

{{Shrine |Name = Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 6.2 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 28.89 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂}} (Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto)
Mizusasara-hime-no-Mikoto (弥豆佐佐良比賣命) |type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime no Yashiro (度会之大国玉姫の社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 20) |Rituals = }} '''Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine''' (度会大国玉比賣神社, *Watarai Ōkunitamahi-me Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is located in the forested area of the Outer Shrine precincts, to the southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiyasaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫}}. [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] == Overview == {{ill|Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社}} is located within the sacred forest ({{ill|Jingū-rin|ja|神宮林}}) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is not directly connected to the main approach (参道) of the precinct, but its entrance can be found along the boundary of the Gekū's sacred grounds. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it ranks fourth, following {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社、大間国生神社}} and {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社}}Sakurai (1998): p. 85. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Kunitama|ja|大国玉命|lt=Ōkunidama no Mikoto}} and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|弥豆佐佐良比賣命}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): p. 37, both considered the {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|地主}} (local tutelary deities) of the Watarai region. It is said that when {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, visited to pacify Ise during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, these two deities welcomed him by creating a bridge with their bows. Notably, Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto’s son, {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦国見賀岐建与束命}}, is enshrined at Watarai Kunimi Shrine. The precinct is surrounded by dense, towering trees, creating an atmosphere of deep shade even during the day. The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}}), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫}} site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]] == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[en:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

The precinct is surrounded by dense, towering trees, creating an atmosphere of deep shade even during the day. The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}}), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫}} site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building.
