ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[en:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

{{Shrine |Name = Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 6.2 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 28.89 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂}} (Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto)
Mizusasara-hime-no-Mikoto (弥豆佐佐良比賣命) |type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime no Yashiro (度会之大国玉姫の社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 20) |Rituals = }} '''Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine''' (度会大国玉比賣神社, *Watarai Ōkunitamahi-me Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is located in the forested area of the Outer Shrine precincts, to the southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiyasaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫}}. [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] == Overview == {{ill|Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社}} is located within the sacred forest ({{ill|Jingū-rin|ja|神宮林}}) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is not directly connected to the main approach (参道) of the precinct, but its entrance can be found along the boundary of the Gekū's sacred grounds. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it ranks fourth, following {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社、大間国生神社}} and {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社}}Sakurai (1998): p. 85. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Kunitama|ja|大国玉命|lt=Ōkunidama no Mikoto}} and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|弥豆佐佐良比賣命}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): p. 37, both considered the {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|地主}} (local tutelary deities) of the Watarai region. It is said that when {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, visited to pacify Ise during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, these two deities welcomed him by creating a bridge with their bows. Notably, Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto’s son, {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦国見賀岐建与束命}}, is enshrined at Watarai Kunimi Shrine. The precinct is surrounded by dense, towering trees, creating an atmosphere of deep shade even during the day. The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}}), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫}} site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[en:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 6.2 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 28.89 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂}} (Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto)
Mizusasara-hime-no-Mikoto (弥豆佐佐良比賣命) |type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}}
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Other Names = Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime no Yashiro (度会之大国玉姫の社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 20) |Rituals = }} '''Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine''' (度会大国玉比賣神社, *Watarai Ōkunitamahi-me Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is located in the forested area of the Outer Shrine precincts, to the southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiyasaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫}}. == Overview == {{ill|Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社}} is located within the sacred forest ({{ill|Jingū-rin|ja|神宮林}}) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It is not directly connected to the main approach (参道) of the precinct, but its entrance can be found along the boundary of the Gekū's sacred grounds. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it ranks fourth, following {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社、大間国生神社}} and {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社}}Sakurai (1998): p. 85. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Kunitama|ja|大国玉命|lt=Ōkunidama no Mikoto}} and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|弥豆佐佐良比賣命}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): p. 37, both considered the {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|地主}} (local tutelary deities) of the Watarai region. It is said that when {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}, visited to pacify Ise during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}, these two deities welcomed him by creating a bridge with their bows. Notably, Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto’s son, {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦国見賀岐建与束命}}, is enshrined at Watarai Kunimi Shrine. The precinct is surrounded by dense, towering trees, creating an atmosphere of deep shade even during the day. The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}}), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇}}’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}}, heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市}} along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫}} site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[en:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]]

{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} |Other Names = Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime no Yashiro (度会之大国玉姫の社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} (October 20) |Rituals = }} '''Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine''' (度会大国玉比賣神社, *Watarai Ōkunitamahi-me Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}. It is located in the forested area of the Outer Shrine precincts, to the southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiyasaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫|12=draft}}. == Overview == {{ill|Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|12=draft}} is located within the sacred forest ({{ill|Jingū-rin|ja|神宮林|12=draft}}) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}. It is not directly connected to the main approach (参道) of the precinct, but its entrance can be found along the boundary of the Gekū's sacred grounds. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it ranks fourth, following {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社、大間国生神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|12=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 85. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Kunitama|ja|大国玉命|lt=Ōkunidama no Mikoto|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|弥豆佐佐良比賣命|12=draft}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): p. 37, both considered the {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|地主|12=draft}} (local tutelary deities) of the Watarai region. It is said that when {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|12=draft}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=draft}}, visited to pacify Ise during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft}}, these two deities welcomed him by creating a bridge with their bows. Notably, Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto’s son, {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦国見賀岐建与束命|12=draft}}, is enshrined at Watarai Kunimi Shrine. The precinct is surrounded by dense, towering trees, creating an atmosphere of deep shade even during the day. The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}}), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社|12=draft}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=draft}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲|12=draft}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|12=draft}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=draft}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft}}’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}}, heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市|12=draft}} along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫|12=draft}} site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|12=draft}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[en:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]]

It is not directly connected to the main approach (参道) of the precinct, but its entrance can be found along the boundary of the Gekū's sacred grounds. It is said that when {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|12=draft}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=draft}}, visited to pacify Ise during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft}}, these two deities welcomed him by creating a bridge with their bows. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫|12=draft}} site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|12=draft}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[en:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 6.2 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 28.89 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂|12=draft}} (Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto)
Mizusasara-hime-no-Mikoto (弥豆佐佐良比賣命) |type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft}}
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} |Other Names = Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime no Yashiro (度会之大国玉姫の社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} (October 20) |Rituals = }} '''Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine''' (度会大国玉比賣神社, *Watarai Ōkunitamahi-me Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}. It is located in the forested area of the Outer Shrine precincts, to the southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiyasaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫|12=draft}}. == Overview == {{ill|Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|12=draft}} is located within the sacred forest ({{ill|Jingū-rin|ja|神宮林|12=draft}}) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}. It is not directly connected to the main approach (参道) of the precinct, but its entrance can be found along the boundary of the Gekū's sacred grounds. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it ranks fourth, following {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社、大間国生神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|12=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 85. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Kunitama|ja|大国玉命|lt=Ōkunidama no Mikoto|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|弥豆佐佐良比賣命|12=draft}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): p. 37, both considered the {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|地主|12=draft}} (local tutelary deities) of the Watarai region. It is said that when {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|12=draft}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=draft}}, visited to pacify Ise during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft}}, these two deities welcomed him by creating a bridge with their bows. Notably, Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto’s son, {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦国見賀岐建与束命|12=draft}}, is enshrined at Watarai Kunimi Shrine. The precinct is surrounded by dense, towering trees, creating an atmosphere of deep shade even during the day. The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}}), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社|12=draft}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=draft}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲|12=draft}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|12=draft}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=draft}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft}}’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}}, heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市|12=draft}} along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫|12=draft}} site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|12=draft}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[en:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]]

Mizusasara-hime-no-Mikoto (弥豆佐佐良比賣命) |type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} |Other Names = Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime no Yashiro (度会之大国玉姫の社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (October 20) |Rituals = }} '''Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine''' (度会大国玉比賣神社, *Watarai Ōkunitamahi-me Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is located in the forested area of the Outer Shrine precincts, to the southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiyasaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == {{ill|Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is located within the sacred forest ({{ill|Jingū-rin|ja|神宮林|12=draft|12=draft}}) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is not directly connected to the main approach (参道) of the precinct, but its entrance can be found along the boundary of the Gekū's sacred grounds. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it ranks fourth, following {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社、大間国生神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|12=draft|12=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 85. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Kunitama|ja|大国玉命|lt=Ōkunidama no Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|弥豆佐佐良比賣命|12=draft|12=draft}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): p. 37, both considered the {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|地主|12=draft|12=draft}} (local tutelary deities) of the Watarai region. It is said that when {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=draft|12=draft}}, visited to pacify Ise during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, these two deities welcomed him by creating a bridge with their bows. Notably, Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto’s son, {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦国見賀岐建与束命|12=draft|12=draft}}, is enshrined at Watarai Kunimi Shrine. The precinct is surrounded by dense, towering trees, creating an atmosphere of deep shade even during the day. The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}}), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=draft|12=draft}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲|12=draft|12=draft}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|12=draft|12=draft}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=draft|12=draft}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市|12=draft|12=draft}} along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫|12=draft|12=draft}} site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[en:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]]

The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}}), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=draft|12=draft}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲|12=draft|12=draft}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|12=draft|12=draft}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=draft|12=draft}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市|12=draft|12=draft}} along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫|12=draft|12=draft}} site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[en:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 6.2 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 28.89 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto)
Mizusasara-hime-no-Mikoto (弥豆佐佐良比賣命) |type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} |Other Names = Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime no Yashiro (度会之大国玉姫の社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (October 20) |Rituals = }} '''Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine''' (度会大国玉比賣神社, *Watarai Ōkunitamahi-me Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is located in the forested area of the Outer Shrine precincts, to the southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiyasaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == {{ill|Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is located within the sacred forest ({{ill|Jingū-rin|ja|神宮林|12=draft|12=draft}}) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is not directly connected to the main approach (参道) of the precinct, but its entrance can be found along the boundary of the Gekū's sacred grounds. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it Ranks fourth, following {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社、大間国生神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|12=draft|12=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 85. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Kunitama|ja|大国玉命|lt=Ōkunidama no Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|弥豆佐佐良比賣命|12=draft|12=draft}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): p. 37, both considered the {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|地主|12=draft|12=draft}} (local tutelary deities) of the Watarai region. It is said that when {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=draft|12=draft}}, visited to pacify Ise during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, these two deities welcomed him by creating a bridge with their bows. Notably, Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto’s son, {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦国見賀岐建与束命|12=draft|12=draft}}, is enshrined at Watarai Kunimi Shrine. The precinct is surrounded by dense, towering trees, creating an atmosphere of deep shade even during the day. The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}}), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=draft|12=draft}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=draft|12=draft}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲|12=draft|12=draft}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|12=draft|12=draft}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=draft|12=draft}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市|12=draft|12=draft}} along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫|12=draft|12=draft}} site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[en:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]]

Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[en:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]]

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] to [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 6.2 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 28.89 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto)
Mizusasara-hime-no-Mikoto (弥豆佐佐良比賣命) |type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} |Other Names = Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime no Yashiro (度会之大国玉姫の社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (October 20) |Rituals = }} '''Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine''' (度会大国玉比賣神社, *Watarai Ōkunitamahi-me Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is located in the forested area of the Outer Shrine precincts, to the southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiyasaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == {{ill|Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is located within the sacred forest ({{ill|Jingū-rin|ja|神宮林|12=draft|12=draft}}) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is not directly connected to the main approach (参道) of the precinct, but its entrance can be found along the boundary of the Gekū's sacred grounds. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it Ranks fourth, following {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社、大間国生神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|12=draft|12=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 85. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Kunitama|ja|大国玉命|lt=Ōkunidama no Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|弥豆佐佐良比賣命|12=draft|12=draft}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): p. 37, both considered the {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|地主|12=draft|12=draft}} (local tutelary deities) of the Watarai region. It is said that when {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=draft|12=draft}}, visited to pacify Ise during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, these two deities welcomed him by creating a bridge with their bows. Notably, Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto’s son, {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦国見賀岐建与束命|12=draft|12=draft}}, is enshrined at Watarai Kunimi Shrine. The precinct is surrounded by dense, towering trees, creating an atmosphere of deep shade even during the day. The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}}), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=draft|12=draft}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|12=draft|12=draft}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲|12=draft|12=draft}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|12=draft|12=draft}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=draft|12=draft}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}, heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市|12=draft|12=draft}} along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫|12=draft|12=draft}} site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|12=draft|12=draft}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[en:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]]

{{Shrine |Name = Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 6.2 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 28.89 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōkunidama-no-Mikoto)
Mizusasara-hime-no-Mikoto (弥豆佐佐良比賣命) |type = {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign |Main Hall = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Other Names = Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime no Yashiro (度会之大国玉姫の社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (October 20) |Rituals = }} '''Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine''' (度会大国玉比賣神社, *Watarai Ōkunitamahi-me Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is located in the forested area of the Outer Shrine precincts, to the southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiyasaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == {{ill|Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is located within the sacred forest ({{ill|Jingū-rin|ja|神宮林|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is not directly connected to the main approach (参道) of the precinct, but its entrance can be found along the boundary of the Gekū's sacred grounds. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it Ranks fourth, following {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社、大間国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Sakurai (1998): p. 85. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Kunitama|ja|大国玉命|lt=Ōkunidama no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|弥豆佐佐良比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): p. 37, both considered the {{ill|Chinjugami|ja|地主|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (local tutelary deities) of the Watarai region. It is said that when {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, visited to pacify Ise during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, these two deities welcomed him by creating a bridge with their bows. Notably, Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto’s son, {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦国見賀岐建与束命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is enshrined at Watarai Kunimi Shrine. The precinct is surrounded by dense, towering trees, creating an atmosphere of deep shade even during the day. The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ({{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of {{ill|Shima, Mie|ja|志摩市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former {{ill|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko|ja|豊宮崎文庫|qq=draft|qq=draft}} site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[en:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]]

revision.comment: 26 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:度会大国玉比賣神社 up to rev 100315426
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 度会大国玉比賣神社 |画像= [[File:Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]][[豊川町 (伊勢市)|豊川町]] |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 29 | 緯度秒 = 6.2 |経度度= 136 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 28.89 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= [[国魂|大国玉命]]
弥豆佐佐良比賣命 |社格= [[式内社]]
[[豊受大神宮]]摂社 |創建= [[雄略天皇]]22年以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= 度会之大国玉姫の社 |札所等= |例祭= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月20日]]） |神事= }} '''度会大国玉比賣神社'''（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）は、[[伊勢神宮]][[豊受大神宮]]（外宮）の[[摂末社|摂社]]。[[豊宮崎文庫|旧豊宮崎文庫]]の西南西の外宮宮域林の山間に鎮座する。 == 概要 == [[三重県]][[伊勢市]][[豊川町 (伊勢市)|豊川町]]の伊勢神宮外宮の宮域林（神宮林）に鎮座するが、[[境内]]の参道とは直接つながっておらず、外宮の宮域に沿って進むと参道口が現れる。外宮の摂社としては、[[草奈伎神社|草奈伎神社、大間国生神社]]、[[度会国御神社]]に次ぐ第4位に位置する櫻井（1998）：85ページ。 祭神は、[[国魂|大国玉命]]（おおくにたまのみこと）と[[弥豆佐々良比売命|弥豆佐佐良比賣命]]（みずささらひめのみこと）の2柱伊勢文化舎（2008）：37ページ。両神とも鎮座地の度会地方の[[地主]]の神である。[[神武天皇]]の代に[[伊勢国造]]の祖・[[天日別命]]（あめのひわけのみこと）が[[伊勢国|伊勢]]平定のために訪れ、両神が持っていた[[弓 (武器)|弓]]をかけて[[橋]]を作って出迎えたという。なお天日別命の子・[[彦国見賀岐建与束命]]は[[度会国御神社]]の祭神とされる。 [[境内]]は、昼間でも暗いほどの鬱蒼（うっそう）とした大木に囲まれている。[[社殿]]は、写真にあるように[[玉垣]]に囲まれている。[[賽銭]]箱は置かれていない。度会大国玉比賣神社の更に先には、外宮末社の[[伊我理神社|伊我理神社と井中神社]]が鎮座する。 == 歴史 == 度会之大国玉姫の社（わたらいのおおくにたまひめのやしろ）と称していたが、現在は『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載されている名称に従い、度会大国玉比賣神社と表記される。[[櫻井勝之進]]は度会大国玉比賣神社に関連して、以下のように記述している。 {{Quotation|一つの社会が[[ヤマト王権|中央の政権]]のもとに統合されその国家の組織の中に組み込まれるならば、そこで祭っていた神々もまた国家至上の大神と何らかの神系あるいは神縁の神々として組織化されることは、たとえば[[古代出雲|出雲]]と中央の関係を描いた[[神話]]にも示されている。 |櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』35ページ}} すなわち、[[磯部 (部民)|磯部]]の有力者であった度会氏に[[トヨウケビメ|豊受大御神]]の祭祀をゆだねた際に、度会氏が祭っていた[[氏神]]も伊勢神宮の中に組み込まれたものと考えられる櫻井（1998）：35ページ。櫻井は、度会大国玉比賣神社の社名からも氏神であったことが推察できると述べている。 創建年代は明らかではない阜嵐健"[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html 度会大国玉比売神社]"<[https://megalodon.jp/2012-0922-0054-36/www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html ウェブ魚拓]>（2012年9月22日閲覧。）が、上述の通り伊勢神宮外宮に組み込まれる以前、すなわち[[雄略天皇]]22年以前から存在したと考えられる。中世にはほとんどの摂末社の[[祭祀]]が断絶した中で、度会大国玉比賣神社は祭祀が途絶えることも、鎮座地が移動することもなく、現代まで受け継がれてきたと考えられている。 社地からは[[陶器]]の残欠が見つかっている伊勢市 編（2011）：364ページ。見つかった陶器は、ほとんどが[[須恵器]]である伊勢市 編（2011）：370ページ。 == 交通 == ;外宮前まで * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]]・[[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄山田線|山田線]]伊勢市駅南口（JR側）より[[外宮参道]]を通って徒歩15分程度。 * [[三重交通]]「伊勢市駅前」バス停より徒歩15分。 * 三重交通「外宮前」バス停より徒歩約10分。 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[伊勢西インターチェンジ|伊勢西IC]]より[[三重県道32号伊勢磯部線]]（御木本道路）を北へ約5分。付近に[[駐車場]]あり、ただし駐車場から徒歩約13分[[神宮司庁]]"[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html 駐車場案内]{{リンク切れ|date=2017年10月 |bot=InternetArchiveBot }}"（2012年9月22日閲覧。）。 ;外宮前から神社まで :外宮前より宮域に沿って御木本道路を志摩市方面へ進み、旧豊宮崎文庫跡の西南西方向へ道を挟んだ場所に参道口があり、苔むした[[石段]]を上ると社殿に到達する。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23年3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * [[神宮125社の一覧]] * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * [[大土御祖神社]] - 一部の祭神が一致する。 == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神宮125社}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:わたらいおおくにたまひめしんしや}} [[Category:神宮125社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[度会大国玉比賣神社]] to [[Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

度会大国玉比売神社]"<[https://megalodon.jp/2012-0922-0054-36/www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html ウェブ魚拓]>（2012年9月22日閲覧。）が、上述の通り伊勢神宮外宮に組み込まれる以前、すなわち[[雄略天皇]]22年以前から存在したと考えられる。中世にはほとんどの摂末社の[[祭祀]]が断絶した中で、度会大国玉比賣神社は祭祀が途絶えることも、鎮座地が移動することもなく、現代まで受け継がれてきたと考えられている。 社地からは[[陶器]]の残欠が見つかっている伊勢市 編（2011）：364ページ。見つかった陶器は、ほとんどが[[須恵器]]である伊勢市 編（2011）：370ページ。 == 交通 == ;外宮前まで * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]]・[[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄山田線|山田線]]伊勢市駅南口（JR側）より[[外宮参道]]を通って徒歩15分程度。 * [[三重交通]]「伊勢市駅前」バス停より徒歩15分。 * 三重交通「外宮前」バス停より徒歩約10分。 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[伊勢西インターチェンジ|伊勢西IC]]より[[三重県道32号伊勢磯部線]]（御木本道路）を北へ約5分。付近に[[駐車場]]あり、ただし駐車場から徒歩約13分[[神宮司庁]]"[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html 駐車場案内]{{リンク切れ|date=2017年10月 |bot=InternetArchiveBot }}"（2012年9月22日閲覧。）。 ;外宮前から神社まで :外宮前より宮域に沿って御木本道路を志摩市方面へ進み、旧豊宮崎文庫跡の西南西方向へ道を挟んだ場所に参道口があり、苔むした[[石段]]を上ると社殿に到達する。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23年3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998年4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == 関連項目 == * [[神宮125社の一覧]] * [[伊勢国の式内社一覧]] * [[大土御祖神社]] - 一部の祭神が一致する。 == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神宮125社}} {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:わたらいおおくにたまひめしんしや}} [[Category:神宮125社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]]
