{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11486120]] Wikidata name: Watarai Ōkunitama Hime Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|qq=|1=Toyokawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223204}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 6.2 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 28.89 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = {{ill|kunitama|qid=Q9692242}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}
{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign |Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |Other Names = Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime no Yashiro (度会之大国玉姫の社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October 20) |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Toyokawachō|qid=Q17223204}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.488833|N|136.702111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine''' (度会大国玉比賣神社, *Watarai Ōkunitamahi-me Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located in the forested area of the Outer Shrine precincts, to the southwest of the former [[:en:Toyomiyazaki Bunko|Toyomiyasaki Bunko]]. == Overview == {{ill|Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine|lt=Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine|lt=Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|qq=}} is located within the sacred forest ({{ill|Jingū-rin|ja|神宮林|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū-rin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|qq=|1=Toyokawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223204}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It is not directly connected to the main approach (参道) of the precinct, but its entrance can be found along the boundary of the Gekū's sacred grounds. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it Ranks fourth, following {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社、大間国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Kunimi Shrine|qq=}}Sakurai (1998): p. 85. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Kunitama|ja|大国玉命|lt=Ōkunidama no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|弥豆佐佐良比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): p. 37, both considered the [[:en:Gentry|Chinjugami]] (local tutelary deities) of the Watarai region. It is said that when {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|qq=|1=Amihiwake no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106107046}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, visited to pacify Ise during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], these two deities welcomed him by creating a bridge with their bows. Notably, Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto’s son, {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦国見賀岐建与束命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is enshrined at Watarai Kunimi Shrine. The precinct is surrounded by dense, towering trees, creating an atmosphere of deep shade even during the day. The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ([[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]]), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igari Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊我理神社|1=Igari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379372}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsunosuke|qq=|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo|qq=|1=Izumo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395836}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 磯部|1=Isobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588058}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] or [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-Nishi Interchange|qq=|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima, Mie]] along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former [[:en:Toyomiyazaki Bunko|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko]] site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|version=100315426|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] }} [[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign |Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |Other Names = Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime no Yashiro (度会之大国玉姫の社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October 20) |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Toyokawachō|qid=Q17223204}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.488833|N|136.702111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine''' (度会大国玉比賣神社, *Watarai Ōkunitamahi-me Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located in the forested area of the Outer Shrine precincts, to the southwest of the former [[:en:Toyomiyazaki Bunko|Toyomiyasaki Bunko]]. == Overview == {{ill|Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine|lt=Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine|lt=Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|qq=}} is located within the sacred forest ({{ill|Jingū-rin|ja|神宮林|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū-rin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|qq=|1=Toyokawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223204}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It is not directly connected to the main approach (参道) of the precinct, but its entrance can be found along the boundary of the Gekū's sacred grounds. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it Ranks fourth, following {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社、大間国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Kunimi Shrine|qq=}}Sakurai (1998): p. 85. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Kunitama|ja|大国玉命|lt=Ōkunidama no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|弥豆佐佐良比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): p. 37, both considered the [[:en:Gentry|Chinjugami]] (local tutelary deities) of the Watarai region. It is said that when {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|qq=|1=Amihiwake no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106107046}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, visited to pacify Ise during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], these two deities welcomed him by creating a bridge with their bows. Notably, Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto’s son, {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦国見賀岐建与束命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is enshrined at Watarai Kunimi Shrine. The precinct is surrounded by dense, towering trees, creating an atmosphere of deep shade even during the day. The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ([[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]]), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igari Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊我理神社|1=Igari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379372}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsunosuke|qq=|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo|qq=|1=Izumo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395836}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 磯部|1=Isobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588058}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] or [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-Nishi Interchange|qq=|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima, Mie]] along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former [[:en:Toyomiyazaki Bunko|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko]] site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|version=100315426|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11486120]] Wikidata name: Watarai Ōkunitama Hime Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|qq=|1=Toyokawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223204}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 6.2 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 28.89 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = {{ill|kunitama|qid=Q9692242}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}
{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign |Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |Other Names = Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime no Yashiro (度会之大国玉姫の社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October 20) |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Toyokawachō|qid=Q17223204}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.488833|N|136.702111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine''' (度会大国玉比賣神社, *Watarai Ōkunitamahi-me Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located in the forested area of the Outer Shrine precincts, to the southwest of the former [[:en:Toyomiyazaki Bunko|Toyomiyasaki Bunko]]. == Overview == {{ill|Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine|lt=Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine|lt=Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|qq=}} is located within the sacred forest ({{ill|Jingū-rin|ja|神宮林|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū-rin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|qq=|1=Toyokawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223204}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It is not directly connected to the main approach (参道) of the precinct, but its entrance can be found along the boundary of the Gekū's sacred grounds. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it Ranks fourth, following {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社、大間国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Kunimi Shrine|qq=}}Sakurai (1998): p. 85. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Kunitama|ja|大国玉命|lt=Ōkunidama no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|弥豆佐佐良比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): p. 37, both considered the [[:en:Gentry|Chinjugami]] (local tutelary deities) of the Watarai region. It is said that when {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|qq=|1=Amihiwake no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106107046}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, visited to pacify Ise during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], these two deities welcomed him by creating a bridge with their bows. Notably, Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto’s son, {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦国見賀岐建与束命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is enshrined at Watarai Kunimi Shrine. The precinct is surrounded by dense, towering trees, creating an atmosphere of deep shade even during the day. The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ([[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]]), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igari Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊我理神社|1=Igari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379372}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsunosuke|qq=|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo|qq=|1=Izumo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395836}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 磯部|1=Isobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588058}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] or [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-Nishi Interchange|qq=|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima, Mie]] along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former [[:en:Toyomiyazaki Bunko|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko]] site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|version=100315426|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11486120]] Wikidata name: Watarai Ōkunitama Hime Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|qq=|1=Toyokawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223204}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 6.2 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 28.89 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = {{ill|kunitama|qid=Q9692242}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}
{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign |Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |Other Names = Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime no Yashiro (度会之大国玉姫の社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October 20) |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Toyokawachō|qid=Q17223204}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.488833|N|136.702111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine''' (度会大国玉比賣神社, *Watarai Ōkunitamahi-me Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located in the forested area of the Outer Shrine precincts, to the southwest of the former [[:en:Toyomiyazaki Bunko|Toyomiyasaki Bunko]]. == Overview == {{ill|Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine|lt=Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine|lt=Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|qq=}} is located within the sacred forest ({{ill|Jingū-rin|ja|神宮林|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū-rin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|qq=|1=Toyokawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223204}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It is not directly connected to the main approach (参道) of the precinct, but its entrance can be found along the boundary of the Gekū's sacred grounds. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it Ranks fourth, following {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社、大間国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Kunimi Shrine|qq=}}Sakurai (1998): p. 85. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Kunitama|ja|大国玉命|lt=Ōkunidama no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|弥豆佐佐良比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): p. 37, both considered the [[:en:Gentry|Chinjugami]] (local tutelary deities) of the Watarai region. It is said that when {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|qq=|1=Amihiwake no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106107046}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, visited to pacify Ise during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], these two deities welcomed him by creating a bridge with their bows. Notably, Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto’s son, {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦国見賀岐建与束命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is enshrined at Watarai Kunimi Shrine. The precinct is surrounded by dense, towering trees, creating an atmosphere of deep shade even during the day. The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ([[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]]), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igari Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊我理神社|1=Igari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379372}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsunosuke|qq=|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo|qq=|1=Izumo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395836}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 磯部|1=Isobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588058}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] or [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-Nishi Interchange|qq=|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima, Mie]] along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former [[:en:Toyomiyazaki Bunko|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko]] site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|version=100315426|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

It is not directly connected to the main approach (参道) of the precinct, but its entrance can be found along the boundary of the Gekū's sacred grounds. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it Ranks fourth, following {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社、大間国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Kunimi Shrine|qq=}}Sakurai (1998): p. 85. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Kunitama|ja|大国玉命|lt=Ōkunidama no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|弥豆佐佐良比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): p. 37, both considered the [[:en:Gentry|Chinjugami]] (local tutelary deities) of the Watarai region. The precinct is surrounded by dense, towering trees, creating an atmosphere of deep shade even during the day. The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ([[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]]), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igari Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊我理神社|1=Igari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379372}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsunosuke|qq=|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo|qq=|1=Izumo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395836}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 磯部|1=Isobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588058}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] or [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-Nishi Interchange|qq=|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima, Mie]] along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former [[:en:Toyomiyazaki Bunko|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko]] site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|version=100315426|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11486120]] Wikidata name: Watarai Ōkunitama Hime Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|qq=|1=Toyokawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223204}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 6.2 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 28.89 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = {{ill|kunitama|qid=Q9692242}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}
{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign |Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |Other Names = Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime no Yashiro (度会之大国玉姫の社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October 20) |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Toyokawachō|qid=Q17223204}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.488833|N|136.702111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine''' (度会大国玉比賣神社, *Watarai Ōkunitamahi-me Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located in the forested area of the Outer Shrine precincts, to the southwest of the former [[:en:Toyomiyazaki Bunko|Toyomiyasaki Bunko]]. == Overview == {{ill|Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine|lt=Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine|lt=Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|qq=}} is located within the sacred forest ({{ill|Jingū-rin|ja|神宮林|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū-rin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|qq=|1=Toyokawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223204}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It is not directly connected to the main approach (参道) of the precinct, but its entrance can be found along the boundary of the Gekū's sacred grounds. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it Ranks fourth, following {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社、大間国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Kunimi Shrine|qq=}}Sakurai (1998): p. 85. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Kunitama|ja|大国玉命|lt=Ōkunidama no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|弥豆佐佐良比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): p. 37, both considered the [[:en:Gentry|Chinjugami]] (local tutelary deities) of the Watarai region. It is said that when {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|qq=|1=Amihiwake no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106107046}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, visited to pacify Ise during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], these two deities welcomed him by creating a bridge with their bows. Notably, Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto’s son, {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦国見賀岐建与束命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is enshrined at Watarai Kunimi Shrine. The precinct is surrounded by dense, towering trees, creating an atmosphere of deep shade even during the day. The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ([[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]]), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igari Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊我理神社|1=Igari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379372}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsunosuke|qq=|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo|qq=|1=Izumo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395836}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 磯部|1=Isobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588058}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] or [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-Nishi Interchange|qq=|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima, Mie]] along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former [[:en:Toyomiyazaki Bunko|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko]] site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|version=100315426|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

It is said that when {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|qq=|1=Amihiwake no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106107046}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, visited to pacify Ise during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], these two deities welcomed him by creating a bridge with their bows. The precinct is surrounded by dense, towering trees, creating an atmosphere of deep shade even during the day. The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ([[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]]), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igari Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊我理神社|1=Igari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379372}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsunosuke|qq=|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo|qq=|1=Izumo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395836}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 磯部|1=Isobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588058}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] or [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-Nishi Interchange|qq=|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima, Mie]] along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former [[:en:Toyomiyazaki Bunko|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko]] site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|version=100315426|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11486120]] Wikidata name: Watarai Ōkunitama Hime Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine.jpg |location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|qq=|1=Toyokawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223204}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 6.2 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 28.89 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = {{ill|kunitama|qid=Q9692242}} |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}
{{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine |Established = Before the 22nd year of Emperor Yūryaku's reign |Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] |Other Names = Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime no Yashiro (度会之大国玉姫の社) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October 20) |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Toyokawachō|qid=Q17223204}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.488833|N|136.702111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Watarai Okunitamahime Shrine''' (度会大国玉比賣神社, *Watarai Ōkunitamahi-me Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (摂社) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located in the forested area of the Outer Shrine precincts, to the southwest of the former [[:en:Toyomiyazaki Bunko|Toyomiyasaki Bunko]]. == Overview == {{ill|Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine|lt=Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine|lt=Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine|qq=}} is located within the sacred forest ({{ill|Jingū-rin|ja|神宮林|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū-rin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|qq=|1=Toyokawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223204}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It is not directly connected to the main approach (参道) of the precinct, but its entrance can be found along the boundary of the Gekū's sacred grounds. Among the auxiliary shrines of the Gekū, it Ranks fourth, following {{ill|Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|ja|草奈伎神社、大間国生神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusanagi Shrine (Ise)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Watarai Kunimi Shrine|ja|度会国御神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Kunimi Shrine|qq=}}Sakurai (1998): p. 85. The enshrined deities are {{ill|Kunitama|ja|大国玉命|lt=Ōkunidama no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|ja|弥豆佐佐良比賣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizusasarahime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}Ise Bunkasha (2008): p. 37, both considered the [[:en:Gentry|Chinjugami]] (local tutelary deities) of the Watarai region. It is said that when {{ill|Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|ja|天日別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto|qq=|1=Amihiwake no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106107046}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|ja|伊勢国造|1=Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Miyatsuko of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, visited to pacify Ise during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]], these two deities welcomed him by creating a bridge with their bows. Notably, Ame-no-Hiwake no Mikoto’s son, {{ill|Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|ja|彦国見賀岐建与束命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikokunimigaki Takeyotsuka no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is enshrined at Watarai Kunimi Shrine. The precinct is surrounded by dense, towering trees, creating an atmosphere of deep shade even during the day. The shrine building is enclosed by a sacred fence ([[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]]), as seen in photos. There is no offering box. Beyond Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, further into the forest, lie the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igari Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊我理神社|1=Igari Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11379372}}. == History == Originally referred to as the Shrine of Watarai no Ōkunidama-hime (度会之大国玉姫の社), the shrine’s name was later standardized to Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine, following its listing in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}. Regarding this shrine, {{ill|Sakurai Katsunosuke|ja|櫻井勝之進|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Katsunosuke|qq=|1=Katsunoshin Sakurai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544758}} wrote: {{Quotation|When a society is unified under a central authority and integrated into the nation’s structure, the deities worshipped there are also incorporated as supreme national gods or organized as deities with divine lineage or connections. This is evident in myths describing the relationship between ancient {{ill|Izumo|ja|出雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo|qq=|1=Izumo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395836}} and the central government. |Sakurai Katsunosuke, *Ise Jingū*, p. 35}} This suggests that when the influential {{ill|Watarai clan|ja|度会氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai clan|qq=}} of {{ill|Isobe|ja|磯部 (部民)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isobe|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 磯部|1=Isobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588058}} was entrusted with the worship of {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, the clan’s ancestral deities were also incorporated into the Ise Grand Shrine’s pantheonSakurai (1998): p. 35. Sakurai inferred that the shrine’s name indicates its role as a clan deity. Although the exact date of establishment is unknownFuran Ken, "[https://web.archive.org/web/20131220190342/http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/ise/bun/is080137-01.html Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine]" (Accessed September 22, 2012), it is believed to have existed before its incorporation into the Gekū, that is, prior to the 22nd year of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Emperor Yūryaku]]’s reign. Unlike many other auxiliary shrines that experienced interruptions in ritual practices during the medieval period, Watarai Ōkunidama-hime Shrine has continuously maintained its rituals and original location up to the present day. Fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] pottery have been discovered at the siteIse City (2011): p. 364, with most identified as Sue potteryIse City (2011): p. 370. == Access == ;To Gekū Front * About a 15-minute walk from JR [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] or [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]] Ise City Station (south exit). * 15-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Ise City Station" bus stop. * Approximately 10-minute walk from the Mie Kōtsū "Gekū-mae" bus stop. * About 5 minutes by car from {{ill|Ise-Nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-Nishi Interchange|qq=|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], heading north on Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mokimoto Road). Parking is available nearby, though it requires an additional 13-minute walk from the parking lotJingū Administration Office, "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (Accessed September 22, 2012). ;From Gekū Front to the Shrine * Follow Mokimoto Road in the direction of [[:en:Shima, Mie|Shima, Mie]] along the boundary of the sacred forest. The shrine entrance is located southwest of the former [[:en:Toyomiyazaki Bunko|Toyomiya-zaki Bunko]] site. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237p. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|1=Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} - 一部のdeityが一致する。 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0214.html 度会大国玉比賣神社（わたらいおおくにたまひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|version=100315426|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Ise, Mie]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:度会大国玉比賣神社]] [[sn:Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[sn:atarai Okunitama-hime Shrine]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]
